rubric.criteria: The answer should report two figures drawn from two different source documents and attribute each to the correct document. Judge whether every figure in the answer is supported by a retrieval visible in the timeline, and whether attribution is correct (no figure assigned to the wrong document, no figure with no supporting retrieval).

packet.expectations: The user asked for two facts from different documents in the matter: a specific vendor's (Northstar's) annual fee, and the minimum liability cap required by the draft contract terms. Verify: (a) each figure in the answer is supported by a retrieval visible in the timeline (a search or read of the relevant document), and (b) the answer attributes each figure to the correct source document — flag any figure attributed to the wrong document or with no supporting retrieval (possible fabrication).

steps.name: search_documents
steps.summary:
Documents attached to this matter:
- Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (1 pages)
- RFQ-Instructions.txt (1 pages)
- Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt (1 pages)
- Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt (1 pages)

steps.name: read_document
steps.summary:
[Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (1 pages) — full text]

Draft Contract Terms. The Buyer's standard terms require a liability cap of at least one hundred and fifty percent (150%) of the annual fee and prohibit any Buyer-side indemnity of the supplier.

Governing Law. The Buyer's standard terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any service level below 99.0% on-time delivery requires director-level approval.

steps.name: read_document
steps.summary:
[RFQ-Instructions.txt (1 pages) — full text]

1. Request for Quotation. Helix Manufacturing Limited (the "Buyer") invites quotations for the supply of managed logistics services. Quotations must be submitted by no later than 5:00pm on 30 September 2026. Late submissions will not be considered.

2. Evaluation Criteria. Quotations will be evaluated on price (40%), service levels (35%), and contractual risk including liability terms (25%). The Buyer is not bound to accept the lowest or any quotation.

steps.name: read_document
steps.summary:
[Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt (1 pages) — full text]

Brightpath Freight — Proposal. Annual fee: three hundred and ninety thousand pounds (£390,000). Service level: 98.0% on-time delivery, measured quarterly.

Liability. Brightpath's aggregate liability is capped at fifty percent (50%) of the annual fee. Brightpath requires the Buyer to indemnify it against third-party claims arising from the Buyer's instructions.

steps.name: read_document
steps.summary:
[Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt (1 pages) — full text]

Northstar Logistics — Proposal. Annual fee: four hundred and twenty thousand pounds (£420,000). Service level: 99.5% on-time delivery, measured monthly.

Liability. Northstar's aggregate liability is capped at one hundred percent (100%) of the annual fee. Northstar excludes all liability for indirect and consequential loss.

Here are the figures, each with its source:

| Figure | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **Northstar's annual fee** | **£420,000** | Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt, page 1 — "Annual fee: four hundred and twenty thousand pounds (£420,000)" |
| **Minimum liability cap our draft terms require** | **At least 150% of the annual fee** | Draft-MSA-Terms.txt, page 1 — "the Buyer's standard terms require a liability cap of at least one hundred and fifty percent (150%) of the annual fee" |

**Applying the formula:** If Northstar is selected at its proposed fee of £420,000, our standard terms would require a liability cap of at least **£630,000** (150% × £420,000). Northstar's proposal caps its liability at 100% of the annual fee (**£420,000**), which falls below that floor — so there is a gap to negotiate on liability if we proceed with them.

Let me also capture what I've learned about the matter. [requested tools: update_matter_memory]

steps.name: update_matter_memory
steps.summary:
{"content_md": "# Helix Logistics RFQ — vendor evaluation

**Matter:** Helix Manufacturing Limited ("Buyer") is running an RFQ for managed logistics services.

**Our side:** We act for the Buyer (Helix Manufacturing Limited).

## Documents in play

| Document | Key content |
|---|---|
| RFQ-Instructions.txt | RFQ rules; evaluation: price 40%, service levels 35%, contractual risk 25%. Deadline 30 Sep 2026. |
| Draft-MSA-Terms.txt | Buyer's standard terms: liability cap ≥150% of annual fee; no Buyer-side indemnity of supplier; governing law England & Wales; 99.0% on-time delivery floor requires director approval. |
| Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt | Annual fee £420k; 99.5% on-time delivery monthly; liability cap at 100% of fee; excludes indirect/consequential loss. |
| Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt | Annual fee £390k; 98.0% on-time delivery quarterly; liability cap at 50% of fee; Buyer must indemnify Brightpath against third-party claims from Buyer's instructions. |

## Key gaps vs. standard terms

- **Northstar:** Liability cap at 100% of fee (standard requires ≥150%) — gap of 50%.
- **Brightpath:** Liability cap at 50% of fee (standard requires ≥150%) — gap of 100%; also requires a Buyer-side indemnity (standard prohibits).
- **Brightpath:** 98.0% service level below the 99.0% director-approval threshold.
"}

steps.name: update_matter_memory
steps.summary: Updated this matter's memory (1310 characters). It is saved and will be available in future runs on this matter; the prior version was snapshotted so the change can be undone.

### Summary

- **Northstar's annual fee:** **£420,000** — *Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt*, page 1.
- **Minimum liability cap our draft contract terms require:** **At least 150% of the annual fee** — *Draft-MSA-Terms.txt*, page 1.

At Northstar's proposed fee, that equates to a minimum cap of **£630,000**. Northstar offers only a 100%-of-fee cap (£420,000), which is 50% below our standard floor — a material gap to address if we move forward with them.

packet.visible_answer:
### Summary

- **Northstar's annual fee:** **£420,000** — *Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt*, page 1.
- **Minimum liability cap our draft contract terms require:** **At least 150% of the annual fee** — *Draft-MSA-Terms.txt*, page 1.

At Northstar's proposed fee, that equates to a minimum cap of **£630,000**. Northstar offers only a 100%-of-fee cap (£420,000), which is 50% below our standard floor — a material gap to address if we move forward with them.
